evals.prompt: Create a new skill that helps our team write consistent ADR files. Put it in the right place for an adopter app using ADSK, keep SKILL.md lean, and include trigger evals.
evals.expected_output: Skill under .agents/skills/ (not root skills/), with what+when description, progressive disclosure if needed, ~20 trigger queries, and skills-ref validate guidance. Mentions near-miss boundaries if relevant.
assertions: Places skill under .agents/skills/ for adopter context (or documents kit vs adopter paths)
Keeps SKILL.md lean / uses progressive disclosure appropriately

evals.prompt: This SKILL.md is 400 lines of rationale and a full slash-command table. Optimize it for token cost without losing the procedure.
evals.expected_output: Moves rationale and command catalogs to references with when-to-load conditions; keeps gated procedure in SKILL.md; applies the would-the-agent-get-this-wrong test.
assertions: Recommends moving non-essential content out of SKILL.md
Preserves core procedural steps in the main skill body

evals.prompt: Our skill keeps firing when users ask for DevOps CI/CD strategy. Fix the description only.
evals.expected_output: Adds a clear Do-not-use / near-miss boundary and suggests trigger eval queries covering that false positive; does not rewrite the whole skill body unnecessarily.
assertions: Updates or proposes description boundary for DevOps/CI-CD near-miss
References or updates trigger eval near-miss queries
Does not expand SKILL.md body as the primary fix

evals.prompt: I only want to rename the skill folder from my_skill to my-skill. Do we need the full optimizer gates?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes a narrow naming fix: align folder name with YAML name (lowercase/hyphens), validate, skip a full rewrite of body/evals unless description or behavior also changes.
assertions: Does not require rewriting the entire skill body or inventing new progressive-disclosure content for a rename-only task
Still mentions validation or a minimal check after the rename
